text: is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: We all know that some people do not seem as emotionally strong as others when life gets difficult.
zh: 我们都知道，当生活变得艰难时，有些人似乎不如其他人那样情绪坚强。

text: difficult.
zh: 艰难。

text: But why is that?
zh: 但这是为什么呢？

text: A study published in 2003 in the journal Science offered an answer.
zh: 2003年发表在《科学》杂志上的一项研究给出了答案。

text: The study followed almost 850 people from birth through age 26.
zh: 该研究跟踪了近850人，从出生一直到26岁。

text: Researchers found that those with a short version of a certain gene were more likely to get depressed after a sad or difficult experience.
zh: 研究人员发现，携带某种基因短版本的人在经历悲伤或艰难事件后更容易患上抑郁症。

text: They found that people with the normal length of the gene were better able to deal with problems.
zh: 他们发现，携带该基因正常长度版本的人更能应对问题。

text: The gene is a transporter of serotonin, a brain chemical involved with mood and desire for food.
zh: 这种基因是血清素的转运体，血清素是一种与情绪和食欲有关的大脑化学物质。

text: The 2003 study captured attention among mental health professionals and popular culture.
zh: 2003年的这项研究在心理健康专业人士和大众文化中都引起了关注。

text: In fact, Science Magazine recognized the discovery of genes for mental illness as the number two breakthrough of the year.
zh: 事实上，《科学》杂志将这一精神疾病基因的发现评为当年第二大突破。

text: The winner was observations about mysteries of the universe.
zh: 排名第一的是关于宇宙奥秘的观测发现。

text: In June, however, other researchers published findings of a large new study.
zh: 然而，今年6月，其他研究人员发表了一项新的大型研究结果。

text: They reported finding no link between the serotonin transporter gene and the risk of depression.
zh: 他们报告称，未发现血清素转运基因与抑郁症风险之间存在关联。

text: The findings appeared in the Journal of the American Medical Association.
zh: 这项研究结果发表在《美国医学会杂志》上。

text: Neil Reich is director of the University of California San Francisco Institute for Human genetics and a leader of the new study.
zh: 尼尔·赖希是加州大学旧金山分校人类遗传学研究所所长，也是这项新研究的负责人之一。

text: He says the earlier study gained so much recognition it became fixed in many people's minds as true.
zh: 他说，早前那项研究获得了如此多的关注，以至于在许多人心中被当作了定论。

text: The National Institute of Mental Health and Kaiser Permanente Northern California also took took part in the latest study.
zh: 美国国家精神卫生研究所和北加州凯撒医疗机构也参与了这项最新研究。

text: The researchers used information from 14 studies involving more than 14 ,000 patients.
zh: 研究人员使用了来自14项研究、涉及超过14000名患者的数据。

text: The scientists examined the data using the same measures as the 2003 study.
zh: 科学家们采用了与2003年那项研究相同的衡量标准来分析数据。

text: They found that the risk of depression was not higher among those with the shorter gene.
zh: 他们发现，携带较短基因版本的人患抑郁症的风险并没有更高。

text: But they also found that stressful events themselves did appear to increase the risk for depression.
zh: 但他们也发现，压力事件本身确实似乎会增加患抑郁症的风险。

text: Neuroscientist Avshalom Caspi, then at King's College London, led the 2003 study.
zh: 神经科学家阿夫沙洛姆·卡斯皮当时任职于伦敦国王学院，是2003年那项研究的负责人。

text: He is now at Duke University in North Carolina.
zh: 他现在任教于北卡罗来纳州的杜克大学。

text: He has criticized the new study as incomplete.
zh: 他批评这项新研究不够完整。

text: He says it ignores evidence that supports the original research.
zh: 他说，这项研究忽视了支持原始研究的证据。

text: Peter Zandi is a genetic researcher at Johns Hopkins University School of Public Health in Maryland.
zh: 彼得·赞迪是马里兰州约翰霍普金斯大学公共卫生学院的遗传学研究员。

text: He agrees that this latest study is not the final word.
zh: 他同意这项最新研究并非定论。

text: He said, after many years of trying to figure out what is going on with the genetic cause of depression, we're still not there yet.
zh: 他说，在多年试图弄清抑郁症遗传成因的努力之后，我们仍未得出最终答案。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 以上就是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: For more health news, go to VOASpecialEnglish .com.
zh: 欲了解更多健康新闻，请访问VOASpecialEnglish.com。
